top_3_stories.headline: Sensex falls 372 points, Nifty ends below 23,950 as renewed US-Iran tension lifts oil and the dollar
Kotak Mahindra Bank in focus as CEO Ashok Vaswani opts not to seek another term; stock among top Nifty losers
Auto stocks lead the decline as crude oil and dollar strength pressure cyclicals

caption:
Three things moved Indian markets today — and here's why they matter.

Monday was a risk-off session. A clean, sourced breakdown 👇

1. 📉 Sensex fell 372 pts (-0.48%) and Nifty closed below 23,950 as renewed US–Iran tension lifted crude oil and the US dollar.
2. 🏦 Kotak Mahindra Bank slipped about 3% after CEO Ashok Vaswani chose not to seek another term; the board has begun a succession search.
3. 🚗 Auto was the worst-hit sector as costlier oil and a stronger dollar weighed on cyclicals, while defensives like pharma and metals held up.
